summary: Bind-shell backdoor exposing /bin/bash on TCP port 1234
details: The package's sole export `s()` (index.js:5-9) opens a TCP server via `net.createServer` listening on port 1234 with no host argument (binds to 0.0.0.0, all interfaces) and pipes every accepted connection's stdin/stdout/stderr to a `child_process.spawn('/bin/bash', ['-i'])` subprocess. Any caller that imports this package and invokes `s()` exposes an interactive shell running as the host user to anyone who can reach port 1234 over the network. The package has no other functionality, no description, no author metadata, and no advertised legitimate purpose — its only behavior is the backdoor.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
